item.title: SPB for action against those responsible for measles outbreak
item.description: Leaders of Socialist Party of Bangladesh (Marxist) on Monday, at a protest rally in Dhaka, called on the Bangladesh Nationalist Party-led government to identify those responsible for the measles outbreak across the country that has killed 409 children....Cultural Event Listings

item.title: Tk 1,000cr fund formed for local green industries
item.description: The Bangladesh Bank has created a Tk 1,000 crore refinancing scheme under the Green Transformation Fund to support environmentally friendly industrialisation and help local entrepreneurs purchase green machinery and equipment......

item.title: Barishal Univ paralysed as teachers go on strike
item.description: Academic and administrative activities at Barishal University came to a standstill on Monday after the teachers began a complete shutdown on the day, demanding promotion....

item.title: BAB fears return of ex-owners under new law
item.description: Bangladesh Association of Banks president Abdul Hai Sarker on Monday expressed deep concern over a provision in the Bank Resolution Act, 2026 that allows former sponsors and owners to return to the banking sector......

item.title: BB allows banks to offer e-loans of up to Tk 50,000
item.description: Bangladesh Bank has allowed banks to commercially launch ‘e-loan’ services of up to Tk 50,000 through a fully digital process, marking the country’s first comprehensive regulatory framework for small online lending......Maps

item.title: Experts stress urgent trade policy reform
item.description: Experts on Monday called for an urgent overhaul of the country’s trade policy, including the tariff structure, exchange rate governance, and export promotion strategy......

item.title: Banks allowed to send money abroad for visa bond
item.description: Bangladesh Bank has allowed banks to transfer of funds abroad for visa bonds and refundable security deposits required by foreign embassies, high commissions, and authorities during visa processing, easing a longstanding difficulty faced by many Bangladeshi visa applicants......

item.title: Visa group country manager Suresh visiting Bangladesh
item.description: Visa on Monday announced that Suresh Sethi, group country manager for India and South Asia, will be in Bangladesh on May 11 and 12, as part of his regional leadership engagement across key growth markets, said a press release......

item.title: DCCI urges river route revival to cut business costs
item.description: Dhaka Chamber of Commerce and Industry on Monday urged the government to prioritise the development of inland waterway infrastructure to reduce the cost of doing business, calling for planned dredging, riverbank recovery and private sector involvement under a Public-Private Partnership framework......

item.title: National Young Health Conference 2026 held
item.description: The National Young Health Conference 2026, jointly organised by the department of public health and informatics, Bangladesh Medical University, and UNICEF Bangladesh, was held at the Super Specialised Hospital of the BMU in Dhaka on Sunday....

item.title: In India, heat-triggered insurance offers ‘some relief’
item.description: Clothes seller Lata Solanki used to face a devastating choice when India’s summer heat hit dangerous levels: risk her health going door-to-door for sales, or lose her income?......

item.title: BB sets 3pc all-in-cost ceiling for short-term trade finance
item.description: The Bangladesh Bank has set a new all-in-cost ceiling for short-term permissible trade finance in foreign exchange, fixing the mark-up at a maximum of 3 per cent per annum over relevant international benchmark rates......Cultural Event Listings

item.title: Bangladesh strengthens maritime partnership with Netherlands
item.description: Bangladesh and the Netherlands are moving to strengthen strategic cooperation in the maritime and logistics sectors with a focus on building sustainable transport systems, modern port connectivity, and a globally competitive shipbuilding industry......

item.title: Oil rises on US-Iran deadlock
item.description: Global stock markets diverged while oil prices rose on Monday after US president Donald Trump rejected Iran’s terms for ending the war in the Middle East......

item.title: TCB resumes truck sales
item.description: The Trading Corporation of Bangladesh resumed its nationwide truck sale programme on Monday to provide essential commodities at subsidised prices ahead of Eid-ul-Azha....

item.title: Prime Bank signs payroll deal with Agni Systems
item.description: Prime Bank PLC has recently signed a payroll banking agreement with Agni Systems PLC, an internet service provider and technology company, said a press release......

item.title: IBBL leads open investment disbursement prog in Pirojpur
item.description: An open investment disbursement programme was held recently in Pirojpur as an initiative of the Bangladesh Bank under its refinancing scheme for marginal and landless farmers, low-income professionals and small businessmen......

item.title: Janata Bank launches operations ‘JanataPay’
item.description: Janata Bank PLC on Sunday launched operations of its integrated digital payment platform ‘JanataPay’ with Dhaka St Francis Xavier’s School and College and Kurigram Government College, said a press release......Maps

item.title: Where it all began
item.description: The 2026 World Cup promises to be bigger than ever. More teams, more matches, more host countries than ever before. What began as a 13-team experiment 96 years ago is now a 48-nation extravaganza, the biggest sporting and cultural event on the planet, which literally billions of people will tune into. With only a month to go, we dive into the history of the tournament. ...

item.title: MTB leads student banking confce in Narayanganj
item.description: Mutual Trust Bank, as lead bank, along with 41 other scheduled banks, recently organised ‘Student Banking Conference 2026’ in Narayanganj to raise awareness among students about financial literacy and savings habits, said a press release......

item.title: Guardian partners with Reckitt Bangladesh
item.description: Guardian Life Insurance Limited signed an agreement with Reckitt Bangladesh PLC to provide insurance coverage for the company’s employees and their family members, said a press release......

item.title: Road minister not sure about comfortable Eid journey
item.description: Road transport and bridges, railways and shipping minister Shaikh Rabiul Alam said on Monday that the government was not fully certain whether journeys during the forthcoming Eid-ul-Azha holidays would be comfortable....

item.title: BRAC Bank, Crowne Plaza host ‘The Motherhood Movement’
item.description: Crowne Plaza Dhaka Gulshan, in collaboration with BRAC Bank, hosted ‘The Motherhood Movement’ on Sunday marking Mother’s Day, said a press release......

item.title: DBH posts Tk 196m net profit after tax in Q1
item.description: DBH Finance PLC has reported results for the first quarter of the year ended on March 31, 2026......

item.title: BSF to get full control of border fencing in 45 days: Suvendu
item.description: West Bengal chief minister Suvendu Adhikari on Monday announced that the process of handing over land to the Border Security Force for fencing the India-Bangladesh border would begin from today, with the transfer to be completed within 45 days, reports India Today....

item.title: Samsung launches Galaxy A37 5G, Galaxy A57 5G
item.description: Samsung Bangladesh has launched Galaxy A57 5G and Galaxy A37 5G on the Bangladesh market......Cultural Event Listings

item.title: Govt plans 4,000MW more power import
item.description: Finance and planning minister Amir Khosru Mahmud Chowdhury on Monday said that plans were under way to import around 4,000 megawatts of new power through the regional grid in India....

item.title: OPPO A6c with 7000mAh battery launched
item.description: Global technology brand OPPO has announced the nationwide availability of its latest smartphone, OPPO A6c, said a press release......

item.title: Deaths surge as mitigation steps fail
item.description: WHILE floods and cyclones dominate the national conversation on climate risk, a quieter killer claims hundreds of lives every year. Science now confirms it is getting worse, and we are not ready. On the afternoon of April 27, a line of Kal Boishakhi, seasonal storms, swept across Bangladesh with little warning. By the time the sky cleared, at least fourteen people were dead......

item.title: Pathological fantasy of political omnipotence
item.description: A SUDDEN hunch turning into excited conviction is perhaps one of the clearest symptoms of the structuralpoliticalilliteracy that Bangladesh, like many countries unable to provide their citizens with a stable and reassuring civic reality, continues to suffer from. The blame, however, cannot rest solely on the impatient citizen. Such impatience does not emerge in a vacuum......Politics

item.title: Closing skills gap
item.description: BANGLADESH today stands at a difficult contradiction. On one hand, the country aspires to become a developed and knowledge-driven economy through industrial growth, digital expansion, infrastructure development and increased global competitiveness. On the other hand, many sectors continue to suffer from a severe shortage of skilled workers, trained.....

item.title: Inflation: governance failure
item.description: Bangladesh today stands at a dangerous economic crossroads, yet the government continues to treat inflation as a temporary inconvenience rather than a full-scale structural crisis. For nearly four years, policymakers have repeatedly promised that prices would stabilise ‘soon’ while ordinary citizens have watched their purchasing power collapse month after month. The......

item.title: Potato farmers need structural protection
item.description: WHEN potato production has reached a record 1.15 crore tonnes in the 2025–26 financial year, farmers in the north are dumping rotten potatoes by roadsides as inadequate storage facilities and collapsing prices leave them unable to recover even the production costs. The crisis has become severe in Rangpur and adjoining regions that account for most of the potato......

item.title: Lightning safety deserves serious policy attention
item.description: DEATH caused by lightning strikes continues to rise steadily, with 11 fatalities having been reported in the first week of May, although lightning strike has been declared a natural disaster. On April 26, lightning strikes killed 14 people, marking the highest single-day death toll so far this year. Media reports and Save the Society and Thunderstorm Awareness Forum say that at......

item.title: Cop among three injured in attack by mugging suspects in city
item.description: The police opened fire reportedly after being attacked by muggers in Town Hall Bazar area at the capital’s Mohammadpur on Monday morning when two muggers and a police member were injured....

item.title: Myanmar complains over pariah treatment in ASEAN bloc
item.description: Myanmar claimed on Monday that ‘discriminatory measures’ are shutting it out of the ASEAN bloc after a summit last week saw the organisation continue to blacklist the country’s post-coup leadership....

item.title: Most pry students not learning even half of what they should: Bobby Hajjaj
item.description: State minister for primary and mass education Bobby Hajjaj on Monday said that most primary level students seriously lagged behind the expected Bangla and mathematics skills....

item.title: Pakistan summons Afghan diplomat over suicide attack
item.description: Pakistan summoned the top Afghan diplomat in the country on Monday after concluding that a deadly suicide attack at the weekend was ‘masterminded by terrorists residing in Afghanistan’, the foreign ministry said....

item.title: EID-UL-AZHA: Sylhet Quomi madrassahs to boycott hide collection
item.description: Sylhet Divisional Quomi Madrassah Sangrakshan Parishad, an association to protect the interests of Quomi madrassahs, on Monday said that they would not collect the hides of sacrificial animals during the coming Eid-ul-Azha due to wretchedly low prices....

item.title: Govt taking steps to attract foreign tourists to CHT: minister
item.description: Chittagong Hill Tracts affairs minister Dipen Dewan has said that the country’s hilly region has immense potential for tourism and the government is taking special initiatives to attract not only domestic but also foreign tourists to the region....

item.title: Three rivers flow above danger level in haor region
item.description: The Flood Forecasting and Warning Centre on Monday said that three rivers in the country’s north-eastern haor region, the Naljur, the Someswari and the Mogra, were flowing above the pre-monsoon danger level....

item.title: Millions risk hunger as Hormuz standoff blocks fertiliser: UN
item.description: Tens of millions of people could face hunger and starvation if fertilisers are not soon allowed through the Strait of Hormuz, the head of a UN task force aimed at averting a looming humanitarian crisis said on Monday....

item.title: Iran war not over, uranium must be removed: Netanyahu
item.description: Iran’s stockpile of enriched uranium must be ‘taken out’ before the US-Israeli war against Iran can be considered over, Israeli prime minister Benjamin Netanyahu told an American broadcaster Sunday....

item.title: Chuadanga mango harvest starts May 13
item.description: THE mango season in Chuadanga district will officially begin on May 13, with the harvesting and marketing of early varieties such as Ata, Guti and Bombai mangoes....

item.title: N’ganj gas explosion victim dies
item.description: One of the victims injured in a gas explosion at Fatullah in Narayanganj died at a Dhaka hospital on Monday while undergoing treatment of severe burn injuries....City & Local Guides

item.title: AKCMH hosts medical camp to raise thalassemia awareness
item.description: On the occasion of World Thalassemia Awareness Day 2026, Amjad Khan Chowdhury Memorial Hospital organised a free medical camp and medicine service campaign on Friday at Chandpur, Pirganj in Natore with the support of Beacon....

item.title: Former dairy cooperative president hacked to death in Sirajganj
item.description: A former president of a dairy cooperative, Sobhan Pramanik, 70, was hacked to death allegedly over a longstanding feud at Ullapara upazila in Sirajganj on Sunday night....

item.title: Govt likely to set up mobile hospitals to tackle dengue
item.description: Health and family welfare minister Sardar Md Sakhawat Husain has said that the government is setting up mobile hospitals to tackle dengue as part of its commitment to ensuring healthcare for all....

item.title: BNP seeks quick probe into AL-era incidents
item.description: The Bangladesh Nationalist Party on Monday urged the International Crimes Tribunal prosecution to progress in investigating its complaint over alleged enforced disappearances, torture and crossfire killings of its leaders and activists during the Awami League regime....Bangladesh Travel Guide

item.title: Coast Guard arrests 9 with 4,000 litres of diesel in Ctg
item.description: The Bangladesh Coast Guard has seized nearly 4,000 litres of diesel being illegally siphoned from a Chittagong Port Authority vessel on the Karnaphuli River in Chattogram and arrested nine members of a smuggling syndicate in connection with the incident....

item.title: World Migratory Bird Day celebrated at NSTU
item.description: Noakhali Science and Technology University celebrated World Migratory Bird Day 2026 at the university on Monday....

item.title: Govt working to build tech-driven smart Bangladesh: ICT minister
item.description: Posts, telecommunications and information technology minister Faqir Mahbub Anam has said that the government under the leadership of prime minister Tarique Rahman is working to build a modern, technology-driven and smart Bangladesh....

item.title: Group art exhibition Sampriti at IGCC under way
item.description: A ten-day group art exhibition titled ‘Sampriti’, marking the 165th birth anniversary of Nobel laureate Rabindranath Tagore, is under way at the Indira Gandhi Cultural Centre in Gulshan, Dhaka....

item.title: No alternative to sustainable sanitation system: CUET VC
item.description: Chittagong University of Engineering and Technology vice-chancellor Professor Mahmud Abdul Matin Bhuiyan said that there was no alternative to building a sustainable sanitation system because the conventional sanitation system was becoming......

item.title: GAU teachers protest against move to appoint outsider as pro-VC
item.description: Gazipur Agricultural University Teachers’ Association has expressed concern and protest against any move to appoint an outsider as pro-vice-chancellor of the university, urging the authorities to prioritise qualified internal candidates for the post....

item.description: Jalkanya has organised a six-day group art exhibition titled ‘Mother in All Women’, marking Mother’s Day, inaugurated at the Safiuddin Shilpalay of Dhanmondi in Dhaka on Sunday....

item.description: Prime minister Tarique Rahman will inaugurate a daylong workshop titled Transforming Higher Education in Bangladesh: Roadmap to Sustainable Excellence at the Nabab Nawab Ali Chowdhury Senate Bhaban of Dhaka University today....

item.title: Unilever, CCC, YPSA sign MoU to advance plastic waste management
item.description: Unilever Bangladesh Limited has renewed its tripartite partnership with Chattogram City Corporation and Young Power in Social Action for a further two years, continuing joint efforts to strengthen the plastic waste management value chain and improve......City & Local Guides

item.title: Two receive Bangla Academy Rabindra Puraskar 2026
item.description: Bangla Academy presented the Rabindra Puraskar 2026 to writer and researcher professor Nripendra Lal Das and Rabindra Sangeet artiste Bulbul Islam at an event in the capital on Monday....
